els_env_001.quote: reply shall be transmitted in response to an ELS request, unless otherwise specified.

els_env_002.quote: or reply is composed of a single Sequence with the ELS_Command code being specified in the first
word of the Payload of the first frame of the Sequence.

els_env_003.quote: Each Sequence may be composed of one or more frames.

els_env_004.quote: quence management apply to ELS frames, Sequences, and Exchanges.  An Accept (LS_ACC) to an
ELS shall terminate the Exchange by setting the Last Sequence bit to one on the last frame of the re-

els_env_005.quote: ply.  An ELS request and the corresponding reply shall be performed within a single Exchange.

els_env_006.quote: The TYPE field for ELS frames shall be set to 01h.

els_env_007.quote: The R_CTL shall be set as specified in table 2.

els_env_008.quote: The R_CTL shall be set as specified in table 2.

els_env_009.quote: The first byte of the Payload (ELS_Command code) of the request or reply Sequence shall be as

els_env_010.quote: shown in table 3.  The remainder of the Payload is ELS unique.

els_env_011.quote: shown in table 3.  The remainder of the Payload is ELS unique.  Subsequent frames, if any, for a re-
quest or reply Sequence shall only contain additional Payload in the Payload field (i.e., the
ELS_Command code is not repeated in each frame).

els_env_012.quote: An ELS Protocol is composed of an ELS request Sequence and, for some requests, an ELS Reply

els_env_013.quote: Sequence.  The last Data frame of an ELS request Sequence with a Reply Sequence shall transfer
the Sequence Initiative to the Recipient in order to allow the reply to be transmitted (see FC-FS-2). If

els_env_014.quote: an ELS request Sequence that has a Reply Sequence is transmitted without the transfer of Se-
quence Initiative, the Recipient shall abort the Exchange and not perform the request.

fabric_login_request_setup_c1.quote: with other Nx_Ports shall not be attempted until the Fabric Login procedure is complete.

fabric_login_request_setup_c2.quote: The Nx_Port shall transmit the FLOGI in a new Exchange.

fabric_login_request_setup_c3.quote: The Payload of FLOGI contains the Ser-
vice Parameters of the Nx_Port, a 64-bit N_Port_Name of the Nx_Port, and a 64-bit Node_Name.

fabric_login_request_setup_c4.quote: vice Parameters of the Nx_Port, a 64-bit N_Port_Name of the Nx_Port, and a 64-bit Node_Name.

fabric_login_request_setup_c5.quote: vice Parameters of the Nx_Port, a 64-bit N_Port_Name of the Nx_Port, and a 64-bit Node_Name.

fabric_login_request_setup_c6.quote: The Service Parameters are as specified for F_Port Login in 6.6.

fabric_login_request_setup_c7.quote: The Service Parameters are as specified for F_Port Login in 6.6.  The applicability of the Service Pa-
rameters to Fabric Login are given in tables 150 and 155.

fabric_login_request_setup_c16.quote: rameters to Fabric Login are given in tables 150 and 155.  The Nx_Port shall assign an OX_ID and

fabric_login_request_setup_c17.quote: set the D_ID to the well-known F_Port address (i.e., FFFFFEh).

fabric_login_request_setup_c18.quote: If the Nx_Port is unidentified, an Nx_Port shall set the S_ID in the FLOGI to 000000h or 0000h || YY.

fabric_login_request_setup_c19.quote: If the Nx_Port sets the S_ID to 000000h, the Nx_Port is requesting the Fabric assign all 24 bits of the

fabric_login_request_setup_c20.quote: If the Nx_Port sets the S_ID to 0000h || YY, the Nx_Port is requesting the Fabric assign
the upper 16 bits, bits 23 to 8, and validate the lower 8 bits, bits 7 to 0, of the N_Port_ID.

fabric_login_request_setup_c21.quote: the upper 16 bits, bits 23 to 8, and validate the lower 8 bits, bits 7 to 0, of the N_Port_ID.

flogi_resp_normal_ls_acc_branch.quote: a) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI, and the Common Ser-
vice N_Port/F_Port bit set to one (Fx_Port) - This is the normal response to a Fabric Login re-

flogi_resp_normal_ls_acc_did_assigned.quote: quest.  The D_ID of the LS_ACC shall be the N_Port_ID assigned by the Fabric.

flogi_resp_normal_ls_acc_did_from_zero_s.quote: the FLOGI was 000000h, the D_ID shall be XXXXXX.

flogi_resp_normal_ls_acc_did_from_partia.quote: YY, the D_ID shall be XXXXYY.

flogi_resp_normal_ls_acc_did_from_full_s.quote: If the S_ID in the FLOGI was XXXXXX, the D_ID shall be

flogi_resp_normal_ls_acc_payload_content.quote: same value of XXXXXX.  The Payload shall include the Service Parameters for the entire Fab-

flogi_resp_normal_ls_acc_next_action.quote: tables 150 and 155.  The Nx_Port may continue operation with other Nx_Ports if the
N_Port_ID, F_Port_Name, and Fabric_Name are the same as in a previous Fabric Login or

flogi_resp_p2p_ls_acc_branch.quote: b) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI, and the Common Ser-
vice Nx_Port/F_Port bit set to zero (Nx_Port) This indicates a point-to-point connection with an-

flogi_resp_p2p_ls_acc_did.quote: other Nx_Port.  The D_ID of the LS_ACC shall be the S_ID of the FLOGI.

flogi_resp_p2p_ls_acc_payload_contents.quote: include the Service Parameters from the FLOGI with all classes marked invalid, a 64-bit
N_Port_Name and 64-bit Node_Name of the connected Nx_Port.

flogi_resp_p2p_ls_acc_name_less_action.quote: N_Port_Name is less than its N_Port_Name, the Nx_Port proceeds to N_Port Login.

flogi_resp_p2p_ls_acc_name_greater_actio.quote: ceived N_Port_Name is greater than its N_Port_Name, the Nx_Port waits for PLOGI from the

flogi_resp_f_bsy_branch.quote: F_BSY with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

flogi_resp_f_bsy_did.quote: The D_ID shall be the S_ID of the FLO-

flogi_resp_f_bsy_action.quote: GI.  The Fabric is busy.  The Nx_Port may retry the FLOGI again later.

flogi_resp_p_bsy_branch.quote: d) P_BSY Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

flogi_resp_p_bsy_did.quote: The D_ID shall be the S_ID

flogi_resp_p_bsy_action.quote: of the FLOGI.  This indicates a point-to-point connection with another Nx_Port that is currently
busy.  The Nx_Port may proceed to N_Port Login after a delay to allow the destination Nx_Port

flogi_resp_f_rjt_branch.quote: e) F_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

flogi_resp_f_rjt_did.quote: The D_ID shall be the S_ID of

flogi_resp_f_rjt_class_not_supported_act.quote: the FLOGI.  The Fabric has rejected the FLOGI request.  The reason code contained in the
Payload determines the Nx_Port’s action.  If the reason code is "Class not supported", the
Nx_Port may originate a FLOGI in a different class.

flogi_resp_f_rjt_invalid_sid_action_from.quote: If the reason code is "Invalid S_ID", the
Nx_Port may originate a FLOGI with a different S_ID:
A) If the S_ID of the rejected FLOGI was 000000h or 0000h || YY, the Nx_Port may select a 24
bit value, XXXXXX, for its N_Port_ID by a method outside this standard and originate a
FLOGI with this value in the S_ID.
B) If the S_ID of the rejected FLOGI was XXXXXX, the Nx_Port may select a value of '00 00
00' or '00 00 yy', or a new value 'XX XX XX' for its N_Port_ID by a method outside this stan-
dard and originate a FLOGI with this value in the S_ID.

flogi_resp_p_rjt_branch.quote: P_RJT Sequence, with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

flogi_resp_p_rjt_did.quote: The D_ID shall be the S_ID

flogi_resp_p_rjt_class_not_supported_act.quote: of the FLOGI.  This indicates a point-to-point connection with another Nx_Port.  The reason
code contained in the Payload determines the Nx_Port’s action.  If the reason code is "Class
not supported", the Nx_Port may proceed to N_Port Login in a different class than used for the

flogi_resp_p_rjt_other_reason_action.quote: FLOGI.  For other reason codes, the Nx_Port should respond accordingly.

flogi_resp_ls_rjt_branch.quote: g) LS_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

flogi_resp_ls_rjt_did_assigned.quote: shall be the N_Port_ID assigned by the Fabric.

flogi_resp_ls_rjt_did_from_zero_sid.quote: If the S_ID in the FLOGI was 000000h, the

flogi_resp_ls_rjt_did_from_partial_sid.quote: If the S_ID in the FLOGI was 0000h || YY, the D_ID shall be XXXXYY.

flogi_resp_ls_rjt_did_from_full_sid.quote: If the S_ID in the FLOGI was XXXXXX, the D_ID shall be XXXXXX.

flogi_resp_ls_rjt_action.quote: tained in the Payload determines the Nx_Port’s action.  The Nx_Port may alter the Service Pa-
rameters based on the reason code and originate a new FLOGI.

flogi_resp_no_response_branch.quote: h) No Response may indicate a delivery error, e.g., error on the physical transport.  The Nx_Port

flogi_resp_no_response_recovery.quote: shall perform error recovery per FC-FS-2.  The Nx_Port may originate a new FLOGI after re-

flogi_resp_self_connection_discard.quote: If the received N_Port_Name is equal to its N_Port_Name, then the Nx_Port is connected to it-
self and this case is outside the scope of this standard.  The FLOGI is discarded.

fabric_login_relogin_sof_c01.quote: Nx_Port detects that the N_Port_ID, F_Port_Name, and/or the Fabric_Name has changed since the
last Fabric Login, the Nx_Port shall implicitly logout with all Nx_Ports and wait an R_A_TOV timeout

fabric_login_relogin_sof_c02.quote: last Fabric Login, the Nx_Port shall implicitly logout with all Nx_Ports and wait an R_A_TOV timeout
period before initiating or accepting communication with other Nx_Ports.  The timeout period shall

fabric_login_relogin_sof_c03.quote: period before initiating or accepting communication with other Nx_Ports.  The timeout period shall
start when the Nx_Port detects the change.  After waiting the timeout period, new N_Port Logins are

fabric_login_relogin_sof_c04.quote: start when the Nx_Port detects the change.  After waiting the timeout period, new N_Port Logins are
required before the Nx_Port may communicate with other Nx_Ports.

fabric_login_relogin_sof_c05.quote: Fabric Login shall only be performed in Class 1, 2, or 3. Since the Fabric may not support all three

fabric_login_relogin_sof_c06.quote: Fabric Login shall only be performed in Class 1, 2, or 3. Since the Fabric may not support all three
classes, the FLOGI Sequence may require retry in a different Class with the appropriate SOF.

fabric_login_relogin_sof_c07.quote: Fabric Login is valid for all supported classes as indicated by the validity bits in the FLOGI LS_ACC

fabric_login_relogin_sof_c08.quote: may be used to attempt Fabric Login or Fabric Relogin.  SOF

fabric_login_relogin_sof_c09.quote: may be used to attempt Fabric Login or Fabric Relogin.  SOF

fabric_login_relogin_sof_c10.quote: Class 6 shall not be used for Fabric Login since the D_ID field requires an Alias_ID multicast ad-

fabric_login_relogin_sof_c11.quote: Selection of the SOF for the FLOGI Sequence is based on the Classes supported by the originating
Nx_Port.  The FLOGI Sequence is transmitted and the appropriate action is specified in 6.2.2.3.  If an

fabric_login_relogin_sof_c12.quote: F_RJT with reason code "Class of service not supported by entity at FFFFFEh" is received, another
supported SOF shall be attempted until the Login procedure is complete or until all supported SOF

fabric_login_relogin_sof_c13.quote: supported SOF shall be attempted until the Login procedure is complete or until all supported SOF
types have been attempted.  If all supported SOF types have been attempted and the Fabric has re-

fabric_login_relogin_sof_c14.quote: types have been attempted.  If all supported SOF types have been attempted and the Fabric has re-
jected all or timed out, the Fabric and Nx_Port are incompatible and outside intervention is required.

flogi_plogi_lsacc_payload_bit_conditiona.quote:
a These fields are only present when the Payload Bit (see 6.6.2.4.19) is set to one. When the Payload bit is
set to zero, these fields are not present in the Payload (i.e., the Payload is 116 bytes long).

flogi_plogi_lsacc_payload_bit_zero_lengt.quote:
a These fields are only present when the Payload Bit (see 6.6.2.4.19) is set to one. When the Payload bit is
set to zero, these fields are not present in the Payload (i.e., the Payload is 116 bytes long).

flogi_plogi_lsacc_large_login_step1.quote: If a Login of 256 bytes or more is required when the buffer conditions of the destination port are un-
known, a timeout may be avoided with the following procedure:
1) issue a Login request with the Payload Bit (see 6.6.2.4.19) set to zero;

flogi_plogi_lsacc_large_login_step2.quote: 2) if the Query Buffer Conditions bit (see 6.6.2.4.14) is set to one in the LS_ACC, issue an RPBC

flogi_plogi_lsacc_large_login_step3.quote: 3) if both the ELS Receive Data Field Size field in the RPBC LS_ACC Payload and the Buffer-to-
buffer Receive Data_Field Size field in the Login LS_ACC Payload are at least 256 bytes, issue
a Login request with the Payload Bit set to one.
